title: Peppery Jamaican Peppercorn Pancakes
description: These fluffy pancakes are infused with a spicy peppery kick and feature sweet snaps of sugar snap peas for added texture and freshness.
instructions: Peel and finely chop the sugar snap peas. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and peppercorns. Mix well.
Add the milk or water to the dry ingredients. Stir until you get a soft dough. If using yeast, mix it in now as per the package instructions.
Knead for about 5 minutes. If dough is too sticky, add more flour; if too hard, add a bit of water.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for about an hour until doubled in size.
Grease a pan with vegetable oil. Divide the risen dough into small portions, roll them into balls then flatten each ball slightly using your palms.
Place flattened pancakes in the greased pan. Cook over medium heat until golden brown on both sides (about 3-5 minutes per side).
Sprinkle sugar snap peas over cooked pancakes for added flavor and texture. Serve warm with a drizzle of honey or a scoop of yogurt.
